2606.30077.title: Online Data Selection for Instruction Tuning via Gaussian Processes
2606.30077.paper_summary: With Large Language Model (LLM) pre-training and fine-tuning shifting its focus from data volume to data quality, quality data selection has emerged as a critical research topic. Existing online data selection methods for LLM training are typically "batch-constrained", limiting optimization to local utility within random batches. To overcome this, we propose GAIA (Global Adaptive Instruction tuning via GAussian processes), a framework that formulates data valuation as a global estimation process. GAIA employs Gaussian Process regression to model continuous utility manifolds across the semantic space, utilizing an adaptive strategy fusion mechanism to dynamically prioritize high-utility samples. By casting the strategy-posterior update as an instance of the classical fixed-share Hedge framework for tracking the best expert, we inherit a dynamic-regret guarantee that characterizes GAIA's robustness under non-stationary quality scores during training. Empirical evaluations on three datasets demonstrate that GAIA significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines like \greats, establishing our method as a scalable and robust solution for efficient instruction tuning.
2606.30077.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLM）預訓練和微調的重點從數據量轉向數據質量，質量數據選擇已成為一個關鍵的研究主題。現有的LLM訓練在線數據選擇方法通常是「批次限制的」，將優化限制在隨機批次內的局部效用。為了克服這一點，我們提出了GAIA（通過高斯過程的全局自適應指令調整），這是一個將數據估值公式化為全局估計過程的框架。GAIA利用高斯過程回歸來建模語義空間中的連續效用流形，並利用自適應策略融合機制動態優先考慮高效用樣本。通過將策略後驗更新視為追蹤最佳專家的經典固定份額對沖框架的一個實例，我們繼承了一個動態後悔保證，這特徵化了GAIA在訓練期間在非平穩質量評分下的穩健性。在三個數據集上的實證評估表明，GAIA顯著超越了如\greats等最先進的基準，確立了我們的方法作為一個可擴展且穩健的高效指令調整解決方案。
2606.30077.author: Jun Wang et.al.
2606.30077.authors: Jun Wang, Quoc Phong Nguyen, Julien Monteil, Vu Nguyen
